It is a shame to a man, who is made after the image of God, not to have control over his tongue, in the moments of passion or rage; let him first overcome and govern his passion, and then trust himself to speak, whether he be in the presence of his family or alone. “Let your speech be always with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.” When we speak, let us…

Jesus who is called Justus (4:11) was also with Paul in Rome, but we know nothing about him except that he was Jewish by race, he had the same name Iysous as Jesus, and he was an encouragement to Paul during the difficult days of his imprisonment (Colossians 4:11).

_An Epistle to the Laodiceans_: "When the epistle [Colossians] is read
among you, cause that it be read also in the church of the Laodiceans;
and that ye likewise read the epistle from Laodicea." (Colossians,
4:16.) The epistles to the Laodiceans is among the lost scripture.

It is highly probable that these two persons were members of the church in Colossae and friends, perhaps converts, of Paul during his stay in Ephesus on his third mission. Some have even suggested that Apphia was Philemon's wife and Archippus, his son. Nothing more is said of Apphia in Paul's writings, but the letter to the Colossians, written about this same time, admonishes Archippus as…
